user-story.so-that: all crates can be developed and built cohesively

criterion.title: Workspace compiles successfully
criterion.details: - Running `cargo build` compiles all 9 crates without errors - Workspace configured with proper member crates

criterion.title: All crate directories created
criterion.details: - polyglot-cli (CLI binary crate) - polyglot-lexer (Lexer library crate) - polyglot-parser (Parser library crate) - polyglot-ir (IR generation library crate) - polyglot-db (Database operations library crate) - polyglot-runtime-wrappers (Runtime wrapper library crate) - trigger-monitor (Trigger Monitor service binary crate) - queue-manager (Queue Manager service binary crate) - runner (Runner service binary crate)

criterion.title: Shared dependencies configured
criterion.details: - tokio 1.41 (async runtime) - sqlx 0.8.6 (database client) - redis 0.32.7 with tokio-comp (queue client) - serde 1.0.140, serde_json 1.0.140 (serialization) - thiserror 2.0.17 (library errors) - anyhow 1.0.99 (binary errors) - tracing 0.1.41 (logging) - clap 4.5 (CLI framework)

criterion.title: Git configuration
criterion.details: - .gitignore configured for Rust projects - Cargo.lock committed for binary crates

criterion.title: CI/CD basics
criterion.details: - .github/workflows/ci.yml runs cargo test and cargo clippy - Local development documented in README.md

subtasks.subtask: Configure workspace-level dependencies with version inheritance
Add workspace dependencies to root Cargo.toml
Configure tokio with features: full, macros
Configure sqlx with features: postgres, runtime-tokio, tls-rustls
All errors must be Send + Sync for async (architecture requirement)
Create .gitignore with Rust patterns (target/, Cargo.lock for libs)
Commit Cargo.lock for binary crates (reproducible builds)
Initialize git repository if not already done
Add cargo fmt --check job (optional but recommended)
Document prerequisites (Rust toolchain, PostgreSQL, Redis, InfluxDB)
Run cargo build from workspace root
Run cargo test from workspace root
Run cargo clippy from workspace root

doc.title: Polyglot Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Manual Cargo workspace setup with 9 crates (3 backend services, 5 libraries, 1 CLI). Foundation-first approach where language integration is built on top of the automated pipeline system.

doc.title: Polyglot Architecture Document
doc.section: ADR-001: Manual Cargo Workspace Over Starter Template
doc.snippet: Decision to manually set up Cargo workspace due to Polyglot's specialized architecture that doesn't fit standard templates. Provides full control over crate organization.

doc.title: Polyglot Architecture Document
doc.snippet: SQLx 0.8.6 chosen for async-first approach and compile-time query verification. Used across all database operations in polyglot-db crate.

doc.title: Polyglot Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Libraries use thiserror 2.0.17 for custom error types. Binaries use anyhow 1.0.99 for error context chaining. Critical requirement: all errors must be Send + Sync for async compatibility.

doc.title: Polyglot Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Foundation epic for compilation. Story 1.1 establishes the Cargo workspace structure that all other stories depend on. No prerequisites - this is the first story to implement.

doc.title: Polyglot Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Rust implementation language, PostgreSQL for IR storage, Redis for queue management, InfluxDB for time-series data. Tokio async runtime required for all backend services.

code.note: This is the first story in the project. No existing code to reference. All code will be created from scratch.

interfaces.note: No existing interfaces - this is the foundation story that establishes the project structure.

constraint.rule: Follow ADR-001: Manual Cargo workspace setup without starter templates
constraint.rationale: Specialized architecture doesn't fit standard templates

constraint.rule: All errors must implement Send + Sync for async compatibility
constraint.rationale: Required for Tokio async runtime across all services

constraint.rule: Use workspace-level dependency configuration for shared dependencies
constraint.rationale: Ensures version consistency across all 9 crates

constraint.rule: Use Rust 2021 edition for all crates
constraint.rationale: Latest stable features, better async/await, improved diagnostics

constraint.rule: Commit Cargo.lock for binary crates, ignore for library crates
constraint.rationale: Reproducible builds for binaries, flexibility for library consumers

tests.standards: Unit tests should be placed in each crate's tests/ directory or #[cfg(test)] modules. Integration tests should be in workspace-level tests/ directory. Target >80% code coverage (NFR-M2). Use standard Rust test framework with #[test] attribute. For this foundation story, focus on verifying workspace configuration and compilation success.

idea.description: Test that cargo build succeeds for all workspace members
idea.approach: Integration test that runs cargo metadata to verify all crates compile

idea.description: Verify all 9 crates exist with correct structure
idea.approach: Integration test checking for Cargo.toml in each crate directory
Parse Cargo.toml and verify dependency versions match specification

directory.description: Workspace root with Cargo.toml defining all members

naming-conventions.convention: Use kebab-case for crate names (polyglot-lexer, not polyglot_lexer)
Binary crates: Simple names (runner, not polyglot-runner)
